raw_papers.title: AI-based eye tracking technology for screening ADHD symptoms in children
raw_papers.abstract: OBJECTIVE: To explore the potential of using artificial intelligence (AI)-based eye tracking technology on a tablet for screening Attention-deficit/hyperactivity disorder (ADHD) symptoms in children. METHODS: We recruited 112 children diagnosed with ADHD and 325 typically developing children. We designed a data-driven end-to-end convolutional neural network appearance-based model to predict eye gaze. The participants completed the eye tracking task on a tablet, which consisted of a simple fixation task as well as 14 prosaccade and 14 antisaccade trials. RESULTS: Two-way MANOVA analyses demonstrated that diagnosis and age had significant effects on performance. CONCLUSION: Our AI-based eye tracking technology implemented on a tablet could reliably discriminate eye movements of the TD group and the ADHD group, providing a potential solution for ADHD screening outside of clinical settings.
